payload.title: Canonical Inbox submit collapses intent, preflight, approval, execution, and confirmation
payload.summary: The Narada-proper task-locus incident and follow-up inbox-submission friction suggest a deeper workflow incoherency: inbox submission is exposed primarily as a low-level execution primitive, while real operator use needs an intent-to-confirmation pipeline. Collapsing these phases allowed an accidental Narada task allocation and later allowed an empty-payload inbox envelope to be submitted successfully while not matching operator intent.
evidence: A local Site/YASB planning discussion crossed into Narada proper task allocation because target locus and mutation authority were not separated from tool execution.
Submitting an observation required several hidden decisions: target inbox, source kind, authority level, payload transport, payload validity, and delivery visibility.
One retry produced env_b384b82a-dc77-4253-8190-70089c85bbfd with payload {}, demonstrating that execution success did not confirm semantic success.
The corrected friction report is env_00c052ea-2fb4-40ff-a7ca-e8e25bd08e39.
The original task-locus incident report is env_796f9b43-a2a9-4b20-ab4b-cb1ec3a7a501.

collapsed_phases: approval when crossing loci or using mutating surfaces
analysis.why_it_matters: Narada doctrine already separates intent, decision, execution, and confirmation. Canonical Inbox submission is itself a crossing into durable intake state, so routine operator submission should not make the human or agent manually preserve that separation across shell commands.

proposal: Keep narada inbox submit as a low-level execution primitive.
Add a higher-level inbox submission workflow for common observations/task candidates, e.g. narada inbox prepare/preview/commit or narada inbox submit-observation.

The higher-level flow should form the typed envelope from operator intent, resolve target locus/inbox, validate enum fields, reject or warn on empty payloads for observation/task_candidate envelopes, and display a preview before mutation.
For cross-locus submissions or Narada proper targets, require explicit approval of the target root, envelope kind, authority level, and payload summary before execution.
After execution, read back the stored envelope and confirm that payload equivalence holds relative to the prepared payload.
Offer optional export/publication in the same confirmed flow so ignored local SQLite state does not become invisible work.

suggested_target.reason: This is a doctrine-level mismatch in a core intake surface: execution is currently too easy to perform without preflight, approval, and read-back confirmation.

promotion.note: Envelope archived; no target-zone mutation was performed.
